About.title: About My Clock
About.intro: My name is Assis Berlanda de Medeiros, I am a SIPITU representative and I developed My Clock with the objective of helping employees control their working hours in a simple, practical and free way.
About.motivation: The idea arose from the difficulty that many workers face daily in correctly checking the hours recorded in the time clock, tracking overtime, validating payments and identifying possible discrepancies in the payroll. Often, when there is an error, the employee themselves needs to do this control manually to request a review with HR.
About.purpose: Thinking about this, I created this application as a support tool for personal control, financial organization, transparency and greater security in checking worked hours.
About.features: My Clock allows recording entry, break and exit times, calculating normal hours, overtime, weekly total and estimated gross amount to receive, following the business rules practiced by the company as faithfully as possible.
About.evolution: My Clock is constantly evolving to offer increasingly faithful control of worked hours and calculations practiced by the company.
About.futureImprovements: New improvements, overtime rule corrections, calculation adjustments and usability enhancements may be added in the future as real needs are identified in the daily routine of employees.
About.contactDescription: For questions, suggestions, calculation rule corrections or to report problems, do not hesitate to contact us. Your feedback is essential to keep My Clock always updated and aligned with the rules practiced by the company.
About.legalDisclaimer1: My Clock is a support tool for personal control and hour checking. Although it seeks to reproduce the company's calculation rules as faithfully as possible, the values and calculations presented should be considered as estimates for personal reference.
About.legalDisclaimer2: In case of discrepancies between the application's calculations and the official payroll values, the values and rules of the company's official system and HR department always prevail. For official payment issues, always consult directly with the responsible HR.
About.legalDisclaimer3: This application was developed independently and has no official link with the employing company, being a support tool created by initiative of the SIPITU representative to assist employees.
About.privacyDataStorage: My Clock stores all data locally on your device (browser) and does not send any information to external servers. Your time data and personal information remain only on your device and under your control.
About.privacyBackup: We recommend making regular backups of your data through the export function available in the application to avoid loss of information.
About.privacyNoDataCollection: The application does not collect, store or transmit personal data to third parties. Your privacy is respected and maintained.

HowItWorks.title: How hour calculation works in My Clock
HowItWorks.intro: My Clock was developed to reproduce as faithfully as possible the calculation rules practiced by the company, allowing each employee to track their worked hours, overtime and estimated gross payment.
HowItWorks.explanation: Below is the explanation of how calculations are performed within the application.
HowItWorks.shiftStartDescription: Each employee has an official shift start time, which may vary according to the team or sector.
HowItWorks.shiftStartBase: This time is the main basis for calculating worked hours.
HowItWorks.clockInRule:
The system always considers:

**the shift start time or the actual clock-in time, whichever is later.**
HowItWorks.clockInMeaning:
This means that:

* if you arrive before your time, no extra payment is generated
* if you arrive late, the calculation starts from your actual clock-in time
HowItWorks.clockInExample:
Official shift time: 07:30

* clock-in at 07:10 → counts from 07:30
* clock-in at 07:25 → counts from 07:30
* clock-in at 07:30 → counts from 07:30
* clock-in at 07:45 → counts from 07:45
HowItWorks.clockInConclusion: In other words, arriving early does not add overtime hours.
HowItWorks.clockOutDescription:
At clock-out, every worked minute is normally accounted for.

There is no rounding.
HowItWorks.clockOutExamples:
* clock-out at 00:03 → counts until 00:03
* clock-out at 00:17 → counts until 00:17
HowItWorks.clockOutConclusion: Every minute after working hours is considered.
HowItWorks.mealBreakDescription:
The minimum unpaid break is 30 minutes.

The system deducts:

**30 minutes or the actual break time, whichever is greater.**
HowItWorks.mealBreakExamples:
* 20-minute break → deducts 30 minutes
* 30-minute break → deducts 30 minutes
* 35-minute break → deducts 35 minutes
* 50-minute break → deducts 50 minutes
HowItWorks.mealBreakConclusion: Never less than 30 minutes will be deducted.
HowItWorks.overtimeDescription:
Overtime is calculated weekly, not daily.

The main basis considers the total hours worked from Monday to Friday.
HowItWorks.normalHoursDescription:
The first:

**39 hours worked in the week**

are considered normal hours and paid with the normal value of your contractual hour.
HowItWorks.overtimeADescription:
After completing 39 hours in the week:

the next:

**4 hours**

will be paid with an additional of:

**1.5x (one and a half times)**
HowItWorks.overtimeAExample:
If your hour is worth €15:

* normal hour = €15
* overtime A = €22.50
HowItWorks.overtimeBDescription:
Everything that exceeds:

**43 weekly hours**

will be paid with an additional of:

**2.0x (double hour)**
HowItWorks.overtimeBExample:
If your hour is worth €15:

* overtime B = €30.00
HowItWorks.saturdayDescription:
Saturday enters as a continuation of the week.

Depending on the total already accumulated from Monday to Friday:

* can enter as Overtime A
* can enter as Overtime B
HowItWorks.saturdayRule: If by Friday you have already exceeded 43 hours, normally all of Saturday will be calculated as Overtime B.
HowItWorks.sundayDescription:
All hours worked on Sunday are paid as:

**Overtime B (2.0x)**

from the first minute.

There is no normal hour on Sunday.
HowItWorks.finalResultDescription:
The application automatically shows:

* hours worked per day
* weekly total
* normal hours
* total Overtime A
* total Overtime B
* estimated gross amount to receive
HowItWorks.finalResultPurpose: These calculations help in personal control, payroll verification and requesting review with HR when necessary.
HowItWorks.importantDisclaimer1: My Clock is a support tool for personal verification.
HowItWorks.importantDisclaimer2: It seeks to reproduce the rules practiced by the company as faithfully as possible, however the company's official system and internal rules always prevail in case of discrepancy.
HowItWorks.importantConclusion: Therefore, any payment difference must be validated directly with the responsible HR.
